violations.text: Spray bottles used for storing chemicals taken from bulk supplies were not marked with the common name of the chemical. Repeat Violation. Correct By: 04/02/2026

Thermometers are lacking in refrigeration unit(s). All cold holding equipment used for time/temperature control for safety (TCS) foods must have at least one thermometer inside the unit that is located to allow easy viewing of the temperature display. New Violation. Correct By: 04/09/2026

Working containers of dry ingredients are not labeled with the common name of the food. Working containers holding food or food ingredients that are removed from their original packages, such as flour, herbs, salt, sugar, and spices must be labeled with the common name of the food. Repeat Violation. Correct By: 04/02/2026
Foods were observed stored on the floor in the storage room. Food must be stored at least 6 inches above the floor. Repeat Violation. Correct By: 04/02/2026

Aluminum foil and tape observed throughout the facility. These materials are not smooth, durable, easily cleanable, and non-absorbent. Aluminum foil and tape must be removed and replaced with materials that are smooth, durable, easily cleanable, and non-absorbent. Repeat Violation. Correct By: 04/02/2026
The facility is lacking thermo labels or similar internal temperature indicating device for the dishwasher. Repeat Violation. Correct By: 04/02/2026

The establishment does not employ a full-time, on-site manager certified in food safety at all times when time temperature control for safety (TCS) food is being prepared, handled, and/or served. FMC expired 11/2024. Per owner/operator, he has taken an approved food safety management course. Owner/operator must be sure to register with RIDOH Center for Food Protection as Certified Food Safety Manager. Application provided. New Violation. Correct By: 02/28/2025

An employee was observed washing their hands at the 2-bay prep sink. Food employees shall clean their hands in a designated hand-washing sink and not in a food preparation, mop, or ware-washing sink. New Violation. Correct By: 02/19/2025
Spray bottles used for storing chemicals taken from bulk supplies were not marked with the common name of the chemical. Corrected On-Site. New Violation. Correct By: 02/19/2025

Working containers of dry ingredients are not labeled with the common name of the food. Working containers holding food or food ingredients that are removed from their original packages, such as flour, herbs, salt, sugar, and spices must be labeled with the common name of the food. Corrected On-Site. New Violation. Correct By: 02/19/2025
Bags of sugar and panko were observed stored on the floor in the storage/prep area. Food must be stored at least 6 inches above the floor. New Violation. Correct By: 02/19/2025

Aluminum foil and tape observed throughout the facility. These materials are not smooth, durable, easily cleanable, and non-absorbent. Aluminum foil and tape must be removed and replaced with materials that are smooth, durable, easily cleanable, and non-absorbent. New Violation. Correct By: 02/26/2025
The facility is lacking thermo labels or similar internal temperature indicating device for the dishwasher. New Violation. Correct By: 02/26/2025
The non-food contact surfaces of the ice machine have an accumulation of residue and other debris. Ice machine is to be cleaned and sanitized. New Violation. Correct By: 02/20/2025

Partially frozen fish was observed thawing in a sink with no water/running water. Time/temperature control for safety (TCS) foods must be thawed in one of the following manners: under refrigeration (41 degrees Fahrenheit or less); completely submerged under running water; as part of the cooking process; or in the microwave if cooked immediately after thawing. Corrected On-Site. New Violation. Correct By: 01/18/2024

Various foods were observed stored on the floor in the storage area and under the 2-bay prep sink. Food must be stored at least 6 inches above the floor. Corrected On-Site. New Violation. Correct By: 01/18/2024
Utensils were observed stored in stagnant water between uses. Utensils are to be stored in a clean, dry location. Corrected On-Site. New Violation. Correct By: 01/18/2024

Cooking utensils observed with the lip surface up. Utensils are to be stored so that only the handles are touched by employees and consumers if consumer self-service is provided. Corrected On-Site. New Violation. Correct By: 01/18/2024
Aluminum foil and duct tape observed in several areas (dishwasher, shelving, wall). These materials are not smooth, durable, easily cleanable, and non-absorbent. New Violation. Correct By: 01/18/2024
The facility is lacking thermo labels or similar internal temperature indicating device for the dishwasher. New Violation. Correct By: 01/31/2024

The walls throughout the facility have an accumulation of soil residue and/or food debris. The physical facilities shall be cleaned as often as necessary to keep them clean. New Violation. Correct By: 01/31/2024
